body.title: DickSimnel.0: OR-powered rack device for autonomous ticket work
body.result: DickSimnel.0 v0.1 operational: proper BaseDevice/BaseShim, atomic claim via next --worker, inference dispatch, Granny routing (CC.0→DickSimnel→OR cascade)
body.description:
DickSimnel (named after the locomotive engineer in Raising Steam) is a persistent OR-powered coding agent and rack device. Started manually, stays running, receives ticket offers from Granny via its device channel (comms://dicksimnel), runs OR inference (wrapping existing MinionDevice), marks ticket in_progress on accept, posts DICKSIMNEL_DONE|ticket=T-xxx or DICKSIMNEL_DECLINE|ticket=T-xxx|reason=... to channel on completion. Granny detects completion by watching the channel.

Lifecycle: start -> drop ~/.granny/available/DickSimnel.0.available.true -> poll for offers -> accept/work/signal -> stop -> remove flag.

Not autostart. Can be interacted with interactively (attach to dicksimnel-0 tmux session). Only DickSimnel.0 for now — no parallel instances.

**Affected files:** devices/dicksimnel/device.py (new, BaseDevice subclass), devices/dicksimnel/shim.py (new), config/profiles/granny.yaml (DickSimnel.0 worker entry), skeleton/registry.py (register device)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; BaseDevice/BaseShim contract; log every lifecycle event and interface crossing at INFO; availability flag managed by shim start/stop
**Scope boundary:** IN: device skeleton + channel offer receiver + OR inference integration + accept/decline/done flow + DickSimnel.0.available.* flag management. OUT: DickSimnel.1+ parallel instances, interactive UI beyond tmux attach, auto-restart by Granny.
**Completion criteria:** python -m devices.dicksimnel starts, registers with skeleton, drops DickSimnel.0.available.true, receives a test offer from Granny channel, marks ticket in_progress, runs one OR inference iteration, posts DICKSIMNEL_DONE; DickSimnel.0.available.true absent after stop.
